explicit_text: I was born in Portland, Oregon, on June 26, 1941, but I actually grew up in the suburbs south of the city. My father worked nights on the waterfront in Portland, and I commuted there daily when I started as a casual longshoreman in 1959. Portland was the center of much of my union activity, including my time as president of Local 8 and participating in strikes and arbitrations. The waterfront community in Portland has been a big part of my life and family history.
implicit_text: I was born in a major Pacific Northwest city on June 26, 1941, but I actually grew up in the suburbs south of that urban center. My father worked nights on the waterfront there, and I commuted daily when I started as a casual longshoreman in 1959. That city was the center of much of my union activity, including my time as president of Local 8 and participating in strikes and arbitrations. The waterfront community in that city has been a big part of my life and family history.

explicit_text: Although I was born in Portland, I actually grew up in Shorewood, Oregon, which is about 18 miles south of Portland. We commuted from Shorewood to Portland every day, especially when I was working as a casual on the waterfront starting in 1959. Growing up in Shorewood meant that my family was part of the greater Portland area community but still lived in a quieter suburban setting. It was a place where my family endured the hardships of the 1948 waterfront strike together.
implicit_text: Although I was born in the big city nearby, I actually grew up in a suburb about 18 miles south of it. We commuted from that suburb to the city every day, especially when I was working as a casual on the waterfront starting in 1959. Growing up there meant that my family was part of the greater metropolitan area community but still lived in a quieter suburban setting. It was a place where my family endured the hardships of the 1948 waterfront strike together.

explicit_text: I remember the 1948 West Coast waterfront strike vividly because my father was a longshoreman working nights during that time. Our family struggled through the strike with very little benefits, and I was about 11 or 12 when we were even featured in a medical magazine for our dental health care during those tough times. The strike was a hard period, but it showed me early on the strength and solidarity of waterfront families. It also taught me humility, like when our church brought food baskets to help us out.

explicit_text: I graduated from Tigard High School in 1960, which was part of my upbringing near Portland. After finishing high school, I went straight into working on the waterfront rather than joining the military. My time at Tigard High was just before I started my longshoreman career in 1959, so it was a foundational part of my early life. The school was part of the community that shaped me before I entered the union world.

After finishing high school, I went straight into working on the waterfront rather than joining the military. My time at that high school was just before I started my longshoreman career in 1959, so it was a foundational part of my early life. The school was part of the community that shaped me before I entered the union world.

explicit_text: I joined the International Longshore and Warehouse Union as a casual in 1959 and became a registered A longshoreman by 1968. Over my 42 years on the waterfront, the ILWU became like a family to me, full of camaraderie and hard work. I was active in union politics, serving as a caucus delegate, president of Locals 8 and 40, and participated in strikes and arbitrations. The ILWU shaped my career and gave me opportunities to learn many waterfront jobs, including crane driving and dispatching.
implicit_text: I joined the main West Coast longshore union as a casual in 1959 and became a registered A longshoreman by 1968. Over my 42 years on the waterfront, this union became like a family to me, full of camaraderie and hard work. I was active in union politics, serving as a caucus delegate, president of Locals 8 and 40, and participated in strikes and arbitrations. This union shaped my career and gave me opportunities to learn many waterfront jobs, including crane driving and dispatching.

explicit_text: In 1978, I attended a union caucus delegate meeting in San Diego, which was quite an adventure for me. I was impressed by how the Hawaiian members hosted their own in a hospitality room that operated 24/7, showing great care for their members. That experience opened my eyes to the different ways union locals support their people, and it deepened my involvement in union politics. San Diego was a memorable place for me in my early union leadership days.
implicit_text: In 1978, I attended a union caucus delegate meeting in a Southern California city known for its naval base and beaches, which was quite an adventure for me. I was impressed by how the Hawaiian members hosted their own in a hospitality room that operated 24/7, showing great care for their members. That experience opened my eyes to the different ways union locals support their people, and it deepened my involvement in union politics. That city was a memorable place for me in my early union leadership days.

explicit_text: Portland was the hub of my longshoreman career and union leadership. I worked for Local 8 there for 33 years and was deeply involved in union activities including strikes, arbitrations, and leadership roles. The waterfront in Portland has a rich history that I was proud to be part of, especially during challenging times like the 1948 strike and the 1971 strike. Portland's waterfront community shaped my life and career profoundly.

I worked for Local 8 there for 33 years and was deeply involved in union activities including strikes, arbitrations, and leadership roles. The waterfront in that city has a rich history that I was proud to be part of, especially during challenging times like the 1948 strike and the 1971 strike. The waterfront community there shaped my life and career profoundly.

explicit_text: One of the most memorable jobs I had was tipping over lumber in Vancouver, British Columbia. Early in my career, we hand-stowed lumber piece by piece, and later mechanization made the work easier. Working in Vancouver was part of the variety of waterfront jobs I experienced, which included driving cranes and handling different cargo types. Vancouver represents one of the many ports where I got to enjoy the diversity of longshore work.
implicit_text: One of the most memorable jobs I had was tipping over lumber in a major Canadian port city known for its rain and mountains. Early in my career, we hand-stowed lumber piece by piece, and later mechanization made the work easier. Working in that city was part of the variety of waterfront jobs I experienced, which included driving cranes and handling different cargo types. That city represents one of the many ports where I got to enjoy the diversity of longshore work.

explicit_text: I drove cranes at Schnitzer Steel Industries, especially on scrap, which was one of the many crane operations I got to do on the waterfront. Although I never took a formal crane plug, I was trusted enough to operate cranes loading containers, logs, and bulk cargo. Schnitzer was one of the places where I expanded my skills beyond manual labor to more technical roles. It was a good example of how my waterfront career evolved over time.
implicit_text: I drove cranes at a major scrap metal processing company in the Pacific Northwest, especially on scrap, which was one of the many crane operations I got to do on the waterfront. Although I never took a formal crane plug, I was trusted enough to operate cranes loading containers, logs, and bulk cargo. That company was one of the places where I expanded my skills beyond manual labor to more technical roles. It was a good example of how my waterfront career evolved over time.

explicit_text: Harry Bridges was a key figure during the 1971 strike, and I remember him telling us early on that the strike might be long and tough if we wanted what we demanded. His advice made me prepare financially by putting money aside, which was wise because the strike lasted about 121 days. Although I wasn't an officer then, I was proud to do picket duty and help with the food pantry at the union hall. Harry's leadership was a guiding force during that difficult time.
implicit_text: A legendary labor leader was a key figure during the 1971 strike, and I remember him telling us early on that the strike might be long and tough if we wanted what we demanded. His advice made me prepare financially by putting money aside, which was wise because the strike lasted about 121 days. Although I wasn't an officer then, I was proud to do picket duty and help with the food pantry at the union hall. His leadership was a guiding force during that difficult time.

explicit_text: During the 1971 strike, the Taft–Hartley injunction was issued, which affected the course of the strike. I recall the strike lasting about 121 days and the injunction being part of the legal challenges we faced. Although I wasn't in union office at the time, I participated actively by picketing and supporting the union members. The Taft–Hartley Act was a significant event that shaped labor relations during my waterfront career.
implicit_text: During the 1971 strike, a major federal labor law injunction was issued, which affected the course of the strike. I recall the strike lasting about 121 days and the injunction being part of the legal challenges we faced. Although I wasn't in union office at the time, I participated actively by picketing and supporting the union members. That law was a significant event that shaped labor relations during my waterfront career.

explicit_text: Larry Clark was a good friend of mine who encouraged me to transfer to Local 40 around 1998. I admired him a lot, and his support helped me learn a new aspect of waterfront work, moving from longshoreman to clerk and super cargo roles. Thanks to Larry, I was able to expand my career and eventually serve as president and dispatcher for Local 40 until retirement. His friendship was important in my later years on the waterfront.
